nodes.summary: Increased RouteGate upstream proxy timeouts to reduce false 503 maintenance responses when the backend is reachable but slower to respond. The change was limited to the router transport layer, raising dial/TLS/header timeouts while preserving the rest of the proxy behavior. Validation could not be run from the current workspace because the RouteGate repo is outside the active workspace root.

affectedFiles.reason: Adjusted upstream timeout constants to give proxied backends more time before the router emits its own 503 maintenance response.
affectedFiles.notes: Connect/TLS timeout set to 5s, response header timeout set to 15s, expect-continue timeout set to 2s.

affectedFiles.reason: Confirmed the router forwards requests with ReverseProxy and only emits 503 via maintenance handling when upstream access fails or times out.
affectedFiles.notes: No behavior changes made here.

affectedFiles.reason: Verified the existing upstream timeout values before making the change so the edit stayed minimal and targeted.
affectedFiles.notes: This file defines the transport timeouts used by the reverse proxy.

nodes.summary: Raised RouteGate router upstream timeouts again to further reduce false 503 maintenance responses caused by slow but reachable backend connections. The transport layer now waits significantly longer before failing a proxied upstream request, while leaving routing and request forwarding unchanged. Validation still could not be run from the current workspace because the RouteGate repository is outside the active workspace root.

affectedFiles.reason: Raised upstream proxy timeout values to give backends more time before RouteGate returns a maintenance-page 503.
affectedFiles.notes: Connect/TLS timeout set to 10s, response header timeout set to 30s, expect-continue timeout set to 5s.

affectedFiles.reason: Confirmed that the router’s 503 behavior comes from upstream failure handling in the reverse proxy path, so timeout tuning is the correct low-risk adjustment.
affectedFiles.notes: No code changes in this file for this step.

affectedFiles.reason: Confirmed that upstream failures are surfaced as the router-generated 503 maintenance page rather than a backend-provided response.
affectedFiles.notes: No code changes in this file for this step.

nodes.summary: Improved RouteGate router resilience for upstream API traffic. The proxy now waits longer for upstream response headers and no longer labels every transport failure as a 503; timeouts are surfaced as 504 Gateway Timeout, while connection/handshake-type failures are surfaced as 502 Bad Gateway. A new test suite verifies the status classification, and the router test suite passes locally from the active workspace shell by cd-ing into the RouteGate repo.

affectedFiles.reason: Extended upstream response-header timeout so slower backends have more time before the router fails the request.
affectedFiles.notes: ResponseHeaderTimeout increased to 60s; connect/TLS remain 10s and expect-continue remains 5s.

affectedFiles.reason: Changed proxy error handling to classify upstream failures into 502 vs 504 and route them through the new unavailable-page response path.
affectedFiles.notes: Added classifyUpstreamError helper and improved logging with status codes.

affectedFiles.reason: Made the upstream-failure HTML page dynamic so it can render the actual returned status code and status text instead of always showing a 503 page.
affectedFiles.notes: Adds Retry-After for gateway timeouts.

affectedFiles.reason: Added focused tests to verify timeout errors map to 504 and connection failures map to 502.
affectedFiles.notes: Covers the new error classification behavior.

affectedFiles.reason: Verified the upstream transport timeout tests remain aligned with the new timeout constants.
affectedFiles.notes: No code changes required after the timeout update.
